page: Amazon Web Services m8a.medium is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. This server features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the single-core performance of the AMD EPYC processor is highly competitive, scoring in the top tier for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core metrics, and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, its multi-core performance is weak, as evidenced by lower scores in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation tests. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, though small-block cached reads perform in the lower quartile.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.medium offers a modern DDR5 memory architecture and high single-threaded compute capabilities for workloads that do not require multi-threaded scaling. The absence of local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses reflects a resource-dense design optimized for network-attached storage. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, text processing, and small-scale development tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 core running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and does not include local storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded compression and text processing, though multi-core and database operations are limited by the single-vCPU constraint. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-threaded clock speeds without the overhead of multi-core scaling. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU at 4.5 GHz, 4 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A single-core general-purpose instance featuring high-frequency DDR5 memory and strong single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.
